tasks.instruction: Fix the race condition in BridgeRun.start(). Currently thread.start() is called OUTSIDE the _lock block, so stop() can be called between is_running being set True and the thread actually starting, leaving an unstoppable orphan process. Fix: move the lines 'cmd = self.build_command(settings)', 'self.command_preview = ...', and the thread creation and thread.start() call INTO the 'with self._lock:' block, BEFORE the block closes. The self._emit('start', ...) call can stay outside the lock since it doesn't need the lock.

Fix duplicate history entry IDs. Currently add_history_entry() uses str(int(time.time() * 1000)) as the entry ID, which can collide if two runs start in the same millisecond. Add 'import uuid' at the top of the file (after the existing imports). In add_history_entry(), replace the line 'entry_id = str(int(time.time() * 1000))' with 'entry_id = str(uuid.uuid4())'. No other changes.

Fix diff truncation cutting mid-hunk. Currently the code does combined[:4000] which can cut in the middle of a diff hunk, producing malformed context for the supervisor. Replace the truncation block (the 'if len(combined) > self._MAX_CHARS:' block) with: find the last newline before position _MAX_CHARS using combined.rfind('
', 0, self._MAX_CHARS), if no newline found use _MAX_CHARS as the cut point, count the number of lines omitted as combined[cut:].count('
'), then set combined = combined[:cut] + f'
...[diff truncated — showing first {self._MAX_CHARS} chars, {omitted_lines} lines omitted]'. This ensures truncation always happens at a line boundary.

Add two new fields to the ExecutionResult dataclass to track attempt number and elapsed time. Both must have default values so all existing construction sites continue to work without changes. Add 'attempt_number: int = 1' and 'duration_seconds: float = 0.0' as the last two fields of the ExecutionResult dataclass, after the existing 'command: list[str]' field. The dataclass is frozen=True — adding fields with defaults at the end is safe.

Track elapsed time in AiderRunner.run() and populate duration_seconds in ExecutionResult. Add 'import time' at the top of the file. In the run() method, record start_time = time.monotonic() immediately before the subprocess.run() call. In the final successful return statement (the ExecutionResult at the end of run()), add duration_seconds=round(time.monotonic() - start_time, 2). Do not add duration to the early-failure or timeout return statements — leave their duration_seconds at the default 0.0.

Add exponential backoff between retry attempts in execute_task_with_review(). Add 'import time' to the imports at the top of main.py (it is not currently imported). In execute_task_with_review(), at the end of each loop iteration where a retry will happen — specifically: (1) after the 'continue' that follows a failed mechanical check, and (2) after updating current_instruction following a REWORK verdict — add a sleep call before the continue/loop: wait_seconds = min(2 ** attempt, 30), then time.sleep(wait_seconds), then log at DEBUG level: 'Task %s: backing off %ss before retry attempt %s'. Place the sleep and log BEFORE the continue statement for mechanical failures, and BEFORE the loop iterates for REWORK.

Fix potential UTF-8 decode errors when reading supervisor output. In the _run() method, find the line 'output = output_file.read_text(encoding="utf-8").strip()' and change it to 'output = output_file.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace").strip()'. This ensures that if the supervisor writes output with invalid UTF-8 bytes (e.g. from a model with encoding issues), the bridge does not crash with a UnicodeDecodeError — invalid bytes are replaced with the replacement character instead.

Add a path escape check in FileSelector.select(). After resolving each path with (self._repo_root / relative_path).resolve(), add a check that the resolved path is within the repo root: use resolved_path.is_relative_to(self._repo_root.resolve()). If this check fails, raise a ValueError with the message f'File path escapes repository root: {relative_path!r} resolves to {resolved_path}, which is outside {self._repo_root}'. Add 'import os' at the top if it is not already imported (check first — only add if missing). Place this check immediately after the resolved_path assignment, before the exists() check.
